summary:
The mammillary body forms part of the floor of the third ventricle as per anatomic studies; however, there are no indications supporting the presence of the ophthalmic division of the third cranial nerve within its floor. Thus,
\boxed{\text{ophthalmic division of the third cranial nerve}} Summary:

The mammillary body is one of the components of the floor of the third ventricle based on anatomical descriptions. There is no indication that the ophthalmic division of the third cranial nerve is located in this area. Therefore,
\boxed{\text{ophthalmic division of the third cranial nerve}} does not appear on the floor of the third ventricle.
